hard.next_step: JUne 24: Shortlisted and we will get informed soon
hard.ais_why:
• The SF Description for SARS captures the RFP scope verbatim: 'The opportunity relates to SARS RFP 33/2025 for the appointment of a service provider to deliver an Integrated Strategic Sourcing and eProcurement Solution System for a period of five (5) years. The solution is intended to modernise and unify SARS' procurement environment by consolidating strategic sourcing and tactical purchasing into a single, integrated platform aligned to regulatory and operational requirements.' Zero AI/GenAI/agentic language anywhere in the buyer's RFP framing.
• Per the Description's 17 Mar 2026 entry: 'EY is fronting with pre sales support - QIT has been done.' EY is the consulting partner fronting the engagement. Limited AI product scope (eProcurement plus Merlin/Merlin Intake only ‚Äî no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze). BD_Engagement is Unengaged. Zero Avoma meetings on file. The RFP itself is regulatory/operational alignment-focused, not AI-modernisation-focused.
• Medium confidence: Description is explicit about the modernisation goal (consolidate sourcing + tactical purchasing) but framed as regulatory/operational alignment, not AI transformation. This is RFP-driven Federal procurement; the score would stay low even with call evidence given the absence of AI products beyond Merlin and Merlin Intake and the buyer's explicit regulatory-modernisation framing.

north_star_verdict.headline: Shortlisted on SARS RFP 33/2025 for a $500k five-year eProcurement and strategic-sourcing mandate, with a responsive but thin channel: SARS's tender office confirmed shortlist status on 24 Jun 2026 yet the deal has never had a buyer call, has no named SARS stakeholder, and is engaged only through EY (Jakob Schleu) and the tender inbox. The close date (28 Feb 2027, 237 days out) is credible for a government cycle, so the problem is not the date, it is momentum and relationship depth while we wait passively on the evaluation outcome.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 27 Feb 2026; 129 days old. Advanced Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted on 22 May 2026 (by Rashi Varma); ~45 days in the Shortlisted stage as of today. 237 days to close (28 Feb 2027). Last buyer touch: incoming SARS tender-office email 24 Jun 2026 (12 days ago) stating RFP 33/2025 is still under evaluation, no next-step date committed. Zero buyer calls ever recorded. Government RFP timing comfortably supports the Feb 2027 date, so the risk is thin engagement and no champion, not the clock.
evidence: Best Case is not defensible on the current evidence: no named SARS stakeholder, no validated champion or Economic Buyer, zero buyer calls, and an AIS Negative read on an RFP that is explicitly framed as regulatory and operational modernisation rather than AI transformation (SF AIS_Why; SF Description). Shortlist status (SARS tender office, 24 Jun 2026) is a genuine positive, but shortlisting in a public tender does not equal selection without relationship depth or shortlist intelligence.

The only live buyer signal is a tender-office reply on 24 Jun 2026 confirming the RFP is still under evaluation, with no committed next-stage date; all outreach is us chasing them or routed through EY, so this is rep-side and partner-side activity, not buyer pull. Recommend holding at Pipeline until a named SARS evaluation-committee contact or the CPO/CTO relationship is activated and confirms next steps.

metrics.narrative: The value targets are documented in the CRM MEDDPICC block (last updated 24 Mar 2026): raise spend visibility from fragmented to near-complete oversight, cut roughly 16% savings leakage from poor contract compliance, roughly halve sourcing cycle times, and improve compliant-buying adoption, with a stated 2.5x ROI and payback under 18 months. These are Zycus-authored, not buyer-quantified: no SARS stakeholder has ever validated a number on a call because zero buyer calls exist on this opportunity. For a revenue agency the metrics logically align to compliance, audit-readiness and cost control, but until the evaluation team confirms the savings baseline they remain a hypothesis. The move is to get the RFP evaluation criteria weighting and SARS's own savings/compliance targets in writing.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is named or engaged on this opportunity: OpportunityContactRole is empty and no buyer call exists. In a South African government tender the commercial authority sits with SARS's accounting officer and Bid Adjudication Committee under public-procurement rules, not a single named executive, so budget control is structurally committee-held for RFP 33/2025. The CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) hinted at a CPO (described as an ex-colleague relationship) and a CTO as budget-relevant, but neither is named, mapped, or contacted. The move is to identify the evaluation-committee chair or the CPO by name and confirm who signs the five-year award.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are the RFP itself, documented verbatim in the SF Description: appointment of a service provider to deliver an Integrated Strategic Sourcing and eProcurement Solution System for five years, to modernise and unify SARS's procurement by consolidating strategic sourcing and tactical purchasing into a single integrated platform aligned to regulatory and operational requirements. That gives us the functional scope with confidence, but the evaluation matrix, scoring weights, and technical/pricing split are not visible, and critically the buyer's framing contains no AI or agentic language, so our Merlin/Agentic-AI differentiation may not map to scored criteria. The move is to obtain the evaluation rubric and reframe our value around governance, compliance and audit-readiness.

decision_process.narrative: The process read from the activity trail is a standard public-tender adjudication: proposal submitted against RFP 33/2025 (follow-up email 22 Apr 2026), advanced to Shortlisted on 22 May 2026, and as of the 24 Jun 2026 tender-office reply the bid is still under evaluation with a finalist/next-stage notification pending ('Once the evaluation process has been' concluded, bidders will be informed). EY is fronting the engagement with pre-sales support (SF Description, 17 Mar 2026: 'EY is fronting with pre sales support - QIT has been done'). What is unknown is whether a demo/BAFO round or committee presentation follows shortlisting and the award timeline; the move is to get that sequence in writing from the tender office or EY.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting visibility exists yet, which is normal at Shortlisted: the deal is well before legal/redline stage. When it reaches award this will be a South African public-sector contract on SARS/National Treasury paper (PPPFA-governed) with government standard terms, a five-year term per the RFP, and likely no Zycus SaaS MSA as the primary instrument. There is no current-contract-expiration forcing function on record and no InfoSec or SOW activity. The move that would create visibility is asking EY or the tender office for the draft contract terms and the post-award onboarding/security requirements for a revenue agency.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is documented and consistent with the RFP: SARS runs fragmented sourcing and tactical purchasing on disconnected systems with no end-to-end visibility, weak compliance control and audit-readiness, and data silos that impair reporting, per the CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) and the SF Description's explicit modernisation goal to consolidate sourcing and purchasing into one platform. For a revenue agency the sharpest pain is governance, transparency and audit exposure, not efficiency alone. The limitation is that this pain is Zycus/EY-articulated, not confirmed by a named SARS owner on a call; the pain owner is presumed to be SARS procurement leadership (CPO) but is unvalidated.

champion.narrative: There is no validated champion. The CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) names a CPO (described as an ex-colleague relationship and internal advocate) and a CTO (technical sponsor) as potential champions, but neither is named, neither is mapped as a contact role, and zero buyer calls confirm any advocacy, access to the evaluation committee, or current engagement in this RFP cycle. A historical ex-colleague relationship is not a working champion until it is activated. Our only live human contact is EY's Jakob Schleu, a bid-driven partner, not a SARS internal advocate. The move is to activate the CPO ex-colleague relationship this month for shortlist intelligence and internal sponsorship.

competition.narrative: SAP is the incumbent ERP at SARS (SF How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_today field), which creates integration pull and ecosystem lock-in that any new source-to-pay platform must overcome, and SAP Ariba is the assumed primary S2P rival given that incumbency, though no buyer call has confirmed the actual shortlist. This is a public RFP, so multiple vendors almost certainly bid, but we have no intelligence on who else made the shortlist or how SARS ranks us. Our wedge is a unified S2P layer that integrates cleanly to the SAP ERP (not a rip-and-replace) with governed compliance for a revenue agency. The move is to obtain shortlist intelligence from EY or the CPO relationship and validate the differentiation against what SARS has actually seen.

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is thin but not dead: forward progress in May (Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted, forecast raised to Best Case), then a quiet passive-wait through June with only tender-office follow-ups; the 24 Jun 2026 SARS reply ('still under evaluation') is the last genuine two-way touch.

items.change: SARS tender office confirmed RFP 33/2025 is still under evaluation; next-stage notification pending
Zycus/EY followed up chasing shortlist next steps (Jakob Schleu, EY)
Stage advanced Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted (by Rashi Varma)
Forecast raised Pipeline to Best Case (by Rashi Varma)
ANA pitch deck shared to EY via Showpad

competitive_position.summary: SAP is the strongest current threat as the incumbent ERP at SARS (SF How_are_you_addressing field), giving it integration and ecosystem lock-in advantage that any new S2P platform must displace; SAP Ariba is the assumed primary S2P rival given that incumbency but is not buyer-confirmed. This remains a positioning hypothesis, not a validated battlefield map: with zero buyer calls we have no shortlist intelligence on who else made the cut or how SARS ranks the field. No competitor was newly named this sweep, so prior threat levels are carried unchanged. Zycus wins by positioning a unified, AI-native S2P layer that integrates cleanly to the SAP ERP with governed public-sector compliance, not a rip-and-replace.

competitors.name: SAP (incumbent ERP)
competitors.quote: Only available info is SAP is the ERP at SARS
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the unified S2P layer that integrates cleanly to the SAP ERP, not a rip-and-replace, with governed compliance and audit-readiness for a revenue agency.

competitors.name: SAP (likely SAP Ariba)
competitors.how_we_win: Unified S2P (Ariba is sourcing-led and bolts on P2P), Merlin agentic automation vs chatbot-style Joule, lower TCO via a single data core, and proven public-sector success to counter incumbent inertia.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: SAP is the incumbent ERP at SARS, and SAP Ariba is the assumed primary competitor for the S2P RFP given market position and integration advantage.
competitors.source: CRM MEDDPICC Competition field and living memory
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin and unified S2P architecture as future-proof versus SAP's bolt-on AI and fragmented modules; emphasise faster deployment, lower TCO, and public-sector references to counter incumbent inertia.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: SARS's stated expectation is a single integrated strategic-sourcing and eProcurement platform aligned to regulatory and operational requirements over five years; our core S2P scope fits well, but our AI/Merlin emphasis is exposed because the RFP contains no AI criterion.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a blind, single-threaded government bid: real progress to Shortlisted, but no named buyer, no champion, and no direct call ever. The win levers are getting inside SARS, reframing value to the buyer's actual criteria, and countering the SAP incumbency.
flags: Multi-thread past EY: our only live contact is EY's Jakob Schleu and the tender inbox, so activate the CPO ex-colleague relationship and the CTO to secure shortlist intel and an internal advocate.

Reframe the value story to the RFP: AIS is Negative and RFP 33/2025 has zero AI language, so lead with governance, compliance and audit-readiness for a revenue agency, not Agentic AI, to fit the scored criteria.
Neutralise the SAP incumbency: SAP is the ERP at SARS, so proactively evidence clean SAP-ERP integration and lower TCO before the evaluation committee weighs lock-in.

items.name: Jakob L Schleu
items.title: Senior Manager, Business Consulting, EY Advisory Services SA
items.risk: Partner-side (EY is fronting the bid); advocacy is bid-driven, not a SARS internal stakeholder.
items.source: SF Task (email) 23 Jun 2026; SF Description 17 Mar 2026

champion_strength.summary: Champion strength is weak. The CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) points to a SARS CPO (ex-colleague relationship, internal advocate) and a CTO (technical sponsor) as potential champions, but neither is named, neither is a mapped contact role, and zero buyer calls confirm advocacy, committee access, or current engagement. A historical relationship is not a working champion until activated. The only live contact, EY's Jakob Schleu, is a partner fronting the bid, not a SARS advocate. The move is to activate the CPO ex-colleague relationship immediately for shortlist intelligence and internal sponsorship.
champion_strength.champion: CPO (unnamed, ex-colleague per CRM MEDDPICC 24 Mar 2026) and CTO (unnamed)
champion_strength.source: CRM MEDDPICC block, 24 Mar 2026; OpportunityContactRole (empty); zero buyer calls

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Our AI positioning is misaligned rather than under-positioned: Zycus is leading with Merlin and Agentic AI (an ANA pitch deck was shared to EY on 24 Mar 2026), but RFP 33/2025 is framed as regulatory and operational modernisation with no AI language, so the AI narrative is over-indexed against the buyer's stated criteria. The stronger position for a revenue agency is compliance, governance and audit-readiness.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 2.0 / Negative. The buyer's RFP contains zero AI, GenAI or agentic language and the AI product scope is limited to Merlin and Merlin Intake, so AI appetite is low; no buyer call exists to contradict the field. Treat this as a compliance-and-consolidation deal, not an AI-transformation deal.

items.detail: No direct access to any named SARS decision-maker; engagement runs entirely through EY and the tender inbox, so we are blind to the evaluation committee.
Public tender under active evaluation limits direct lobbying; we depend on EY-mediated intel and any pre-existing CPO relationship for visibility.
SAP is the incumbent ERP; failure to prove clean SAP-ERP integration hands the incumbent a lock-in advantage.
Price-sensitive government buyer on a five-year mandate with no buyer-validated ROI; commercial evaluation is explicitly benchmarked against SAP/others.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is thin and passive: SARS is responsive on the tender inbox (23-24 Jun 2026) but there is no direct stakeholder, no call, and no dated next step; we are waiting on the evaluation outcome.
items: Last two-way touch was SARS's 24 Jun 2026 tender-office reply confirming the RFP is still under evaluation, no date committed.
No buyer meeting has ever been held on this opportunity.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We're shortlisted on SARS RFP 33/2025 for a $500k five-year eProcurement and strategic-sourcing mandate, but the deal is blind: zero buyer calls, no named SARS stakeholder, engagement only through EY and the tender inbox, an AIS-Negative fit, and a Merlin/AI wedge misaligned to an RFP framed as regulatory modernisation.

win_position.text: Sits low against its Shortlisted cap (~70) because AIS is Negative (2.0), no SARS stakeholder is named or engaged, and the entire read is CRM and partner-sourced with zero buyer calls (SF AIS_Why; empty OpportunityContactRole).

Genuine forward progress: SARS advanced us Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted on 22 May 2026 and the tender office confirmed shortlist status on 24 Jun, so we are in the live evaluation set for a $500k five-year award.

Our AI/Merlin differentiation is misaligned to the buyer's stated criteria: RFP 33/2025 is framed as regulatory and operational modernisation with zero AI language (SF Description), so the agentic wedge may not score.
SAP is the incumbent ERP at SARS (SF How_are_you_addressing field), creating integration and ecosystem lock-in pull we have not countered with any buyer-validated proof.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is passive: since the 22 May 2026 stage move, the only activity is us chasing the tender office, whose 24 Jun reply was 'RFP 33/2025 is still under evaluation' with no date committed.
No buyer meeting has ever been recorded; engagement runs entirely through EY (Jakob Schleu) and the tender inbox, not a SARS decision-maker.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is one-sided: SARS shortlisted us but made no dated next-step commitment; their 24 Jun 2026 note only promises to inform bidders once the evaluation concludes.
The buyer is responsive: SARS tender office replied to our follow-ups on 23 and 24 Jun 2026, so the channel is live even if thin.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded and blind: no named SARS stakeholder, no validated champion, and no shortlist intel means we cannot see who else made the cut or how we rank.
AIS Negative plus an AI-silent RFP means our core Merlin story may not resonate; the win depends on a compliance and governance framing we have not yet tailored (SF AIS_Why).

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer call ever; only tender-office follow-ups since the 22 May stage move; 24 Jun reply committed no date.

customer_commitment.evidence: Responsive tender inbox but no named stakeholder and no dated next step (SF Task 24 Jun 2026).

deal_risk.evidence: Single-threaded via EY, no champion, SAP incumbent ERP, AIS Negative on an AI-silent RFP.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer preference expressed; the only buyer communication is a tender-office notice that RFP 33/2025 is still under evaluation (24 Jun 2026).

business_case.evidence: Value targets (16% leakage cut, ~half sourcing-cycle time, 2.5x ROI) are documented in CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) but Zycus-authored, not buyer-quantified; no call has validated them.

critical_signals.text: The last real exchange was SARS's 24 Jun 2026 tender-office email confirming we are shortlisted but still under evaluation, with no next-step date committed.
EY's Jakob Schleu is fronting the bid as our only live contact; no SARS internal decision-maker is engaged or even named.
SAP is the incumbent ERP at SARS, so any new source-to-pay platform must prove clean integration and justify displacing an entrenched vendor.
At $500k over five years against a price-sensitive government buyer with an incumbent ERP, we have no buyer-validated ROI and no confirmed budget owner.

scope_change.detail: Amount settled at $500k (450k to 500k on 18 Apr 2026) with product scope unchanged; no narrowing this cycle.

items.action: Ask the relevant stakeholder for the RFP 33/2025 evaluation timeline and next-stage criteria in writing.

items.expected_effect: Restores visibility into the decision timeline and unblocks planning toward the Feb 2027 close.

items.action: Activate the SARS CPO ex-colleague relationship for shortlist intel and an internal advocate.
items.trigger: CRM MEDDPICC names a CPO ex-colleague relationship but no SARS contact is engaged (24 Mar 2026).
items.expected_effect: Converts a dormant relationship into a live champion and gives us insight into how we rank against the field.

items.action: Rebuild the value story around compliance, governance and audit-readiness for the SARS evaluation.
items.trigger: AIS Negative and RFP 33/2025 has zero AI language while we pitched an ANA/Agentic AI deck (24 Mar 2026).
items.expected_effect: Aligns our positioning to the scored criteria and lifts win probability with an AI-resistant buyer.

items.action: Run a competitive review versus SAP and prepare clean SAP-ERP integration proof.
items.trigger: SAP is the incumbent ERP at SARS and no competitive review has run in 30 days.
items.expected_effect: Neutralises the incumbent lock-in argument before the committee weighs integration risk.

items.action: Identify and name the SARS evaluation-committee owner and map the award decision process.
items.trigger: No Economic Buyer or committee contact is named; adjudication is committee-held.
items.expected_effect: Closes the biggest power gap so we can direct proof and commercials at the real decision authority.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 35 is carried by business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: momentum 50 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 11 is anchored on the stage win (35), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: SOUTH AFRICAN REVENUE SERVICES ( SARS ) — Shortlisted · $500K · closes 28 Feb
cro_panel.intro: We're shortlisted on SARS RFP 33/2025 for a $500k five-year eProcurement and strategic-sourcing mandate, but the deal is blind: zero buyer calls, no named SARS stakeholder, engagement only through EY and the tender inbox, an AIS-Negative fit, and a Merlin/AI wedge misaligned to an RFP framed as regulatory modernisation.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Sits low against its Shortlisted cap (~70) because AIS is Negative (2.0), no SARS stakeholder is named or engaged, and the entire read is CRM and partner-sourced with zero buyer calls (SF AIS_Why; empty OpportunityContactRole).

Genuine forward progress: SARS advanced us Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted on 22 May 2026 and the tender office confirmed shortlist status on 24 Jun, so we are in the live evaluation set for a $500k five-year award.

Our AI/Merlin differentiation is misaligned to the buyer's stated criteria: RFP 33/2025 is framed as regulatory and operational modernisation with zero AI language (SF Description), so the agentic wedge may not score.
SAP is the incumbent ERP at SARS (SF How_are_you_addressing field), creating integration and ecosystem lock-in pull we have not countered with any buyer-validated proof.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 35.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum is passive: since the 22 May 2026 stage move, the only activity is us chasing the tender office, whose 24 Jun reply was 'RFP 33/2025 is still under evaluation' with no date committed.
No buyer meeting has ever been recorded; engagement runs entirely through EY (Jakob Schleu) and the tender inbox, not a SARS decision-maker.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Single-threaded and blind: no named SARS stakeholder, no validated champion, and no shortlist intel means we cannot see who else made the cut or how we rank.
AIS Negative plus an AI-silent RFP means our core Merlin story may not resonate; the win depends on a compliance and governance framing we have not yet tailored (SF AIS_Why).

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: SAP is the strongest current threat as the incumbent ERP at SARS (SF How_are_you_addressing field), giving it integration and ecosystem lock-in advantage that any new S2P platform must displace; SAP Ariba is the assumed primary S2P rival…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Commitment is one-sided: SARS shortlisted us but made no dated next-step commitment; their 24 Jun 2026 note only promises to inform bidders once the evaluation concludes.
The buyer is responsive: SARS tender office replied to our follow-ups on 23 and 24 Jun 2026, so the channel is live even if thin.

Activate the SARS CPO ex-colleague relationship for shortlist intel and an internal advocate. (by 20 Jul)

gaps: Zero buyer calls exist on this opportunity (opp, account, and attendee-email discovery all empty), so all buyer articulation is CRM/partner-sourced.
OpportunityContactRole is empty: no named SARS stakeholder is mapped; CPO/CTO are role-only mentions in CRM MEDDPICC.
Economic Buyer / evaluation-committee owner is unmapped.
Competitive shortlist for RFP 33/2025 is unknown beyond the SAP incumbent read.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: "EY's Jakob Schleu")
